manifest.name: Ops
manifest.description: Makemind Ops — per-project operations console for tasks, processes, knowledge, members, skills, profiles, philosophies, and bundles. vibe_studio built-in app: each Ops tab binds a single project directory (standard newProjectInActive / openProjectInActive wiring); members, tasks, skills, processes, philosophies, profiles, and knowledge live inside the project. The seed ships only the shared `ops.admin` agent and operations-manual knowledge that every project reuses.

sources.title: Ops — identity, project layout, surfaces
sources.description: Makemind Ops identity, project structure, sidebar, agent / lifecycle wiring. Entry point for both external LLM clients and the host chat manager.

documents.title: Ops overview — purpose + positioning
documents.content:
# Ops overview

Makemind Ops is a per-project operations console (vibe_studio built-in app `makemind_ops`).

## Identity
- One Ops tab = one project directory (same standard wiring as App Builder / Scene Builder).
- On first entry the wizard forces a New / Open selection.
- Operations data (members · tasks · skills · processes · profiles · philosophies · knowledge) all live inside the bound project.
- Seed assets = shared `ops.admin` agent + operations-manual knowledge only. No operations data.

## 13-route sidebar
Home · Workspaces · Members · Tasks · Processes · Knowledge · Skills · Profiles · Philosophies · Bundles · Activity · Audit · About.
(Chat and Settings are owned by the host chrome — `chromeBridge.activeChatAgentId` + domain panel in the host Settings dialog.)

## 5 facades + 8 capabilities
- Knowledge facade × 5 — Facts · Skills · Profiles · Philosophies · Knowledge
- Capability stack × 8 — LLM · Channel · Browser · Ingest · Form · LocalKV · ToolDispatcher · Knowledge

Runs in hybrid mode on top of the vibe_studio backbone (`KernelApp.system`). When the project changes, ensureBoot rebinds to the new root.

documents.title: Ops project layout — one bound project per Ops tab
documents.content:
# Project layout

Ops uses standard vibe_studio built-in wiring — one Ops tab = one project.

## Forced entry
- Empty tab = wizard (`New project` / `Open project` two buttons).
- User selection → `chromeBridge.newProjectInActive` / `openProjectInActive` → `_bindProject` → KnowledgeInit boot.
- The seed contains only the policy layer (shared `ops.admin` + operations manual). No data (organizations · projects · members · tasks · etc.).

## Project folder layout
```
<currentProject>/                — Ops project root (selected by user via New / Open)
  project.opsproj               — marker (checked by `isOpsProjectDir`)
  _system/                      — reserved (home workspace for `ops.admin`)
  members/                      — agent · person (member.yaml)
  philosophies/                 — editorial principles etc. (yaml)
  processes/                    — multi-step workflow definitions + execution history
  profiles/                     — agent persona definitions (yaml)
  skills/                       — MCP tool definitions + action bodies (yaml)
  tasks/                        — single-shot task queue
  knowledge/                    — Fact · Pattern · Summary · files
  auth/                         — external channel credentials (Keychain · encrypted)
```

## Shared vs per-project
- **Shared (seed)** — `ops.admin` agent + operations manual (`studio://knowledge/ops_*`).
- **Per-project** — assets in the folders above. All operations data added by the user.

## Project switching
- Project Header `Open` or wizard `Open project` — select a different directory.
- `_bindProject` re-invoked → KnowledgeInit rebinds to the new root.

documents.title: Ops lifecycle wiring — chrome bridge contract
documents.content:
# Lifecycle wiring

The Ops shell follows only the standard host chrome wiring (same contract as App Builder / Scene Builder).

## chrome bridge slots
- `chromeBridge.newProjectInActive` — `_newProject({name, parent})` — creates directory + `applyOpsProjectSeed` + `_bindProject`.
- `chromeBridge.openProjectInActive` — `_openProject(path)` — validates with `isOpsProjectDir`, then `_bindProject`.
- `chromeBridge.closeProjectInActive` — `_closeProject()` — `_currentProject = null` + `resetBootCache`.
- `chromeBridge.lifecycleState` — pushes `DomainLifecycleState(hasProject, projectName)` ("No project open" on Home, `basename(dir)` when bound).

## Active-tab gate
IndexedStack eagerly mounts all tab bodies, so the chrome bridge is a process-wide singleton. If two Ops tabs are mounted simultaneously, the later one can capture the slot — all chrome bridge writes must pass the `_isActiveTab` check (`BuiltInAppRegistry.instance.activeContext?.bundlePath == widget.bundlePath`) before executing.

## Boot cache
`OpsBuiltInApp.ensureBoot({backbone, currentProject})` — process-wide cache.
- When `currentProject` changes, a new boot is triggered and the previous boot's facade is disposed.
- Same project + same backbone = cached instance returned.
- `resetBootCache` (closeProject path) registers dispose → the next ensureBoot creates a fresh KnowledgeInit.

documents.title: Ops agents — shared `ops.admin` (seed) + per-project members
documents.content:
# Agents

## `ops.admin` (shared, seed)
- ID: `ops.admin` — registered in the seed's `manifest.agents[]`, shared across all Ops projects.
- Display: "Ops Admin"
- Role: `manager` — vibe_studio's chat routing convention routes chat entry via `role: manager`.
- Tool allowlist = empty list (wildcard). Can call all tools exposed on the host endpoint.
- Host chat = automatically routed to `ops.admin` via `chromeBridge.activeChatAgentId = 'ops.admin'` when the Ops tab is active.
- Auxiliary home workspace = the bound project's `_system/` directory (ensured at boot).

## Project members (per project)
- Defined in `members/*.yaml` within the bound project.
- type = `agent` (LLM) or `person` (external user).
- Agent members have their own LLM model, skill allowlist, and persona (profile).
- Separate from the seed's `ops.admin` — project members participate only in that project's operations flow (publish · review · ads · etc.).

## Delegation calls
- Single-turn member agent call = `agent_ask({agentId:'<member_id>', message})`.
- Batch work for a member agent = `task_create({title, skill, args, assigneeId:'<member_id>'})` or `process_start`.

sources.title: Ops delegation patterns — manager → member / skill / process / task
sources.description: Decision tree for how the manager (ops.admin) delegates or executes when it receives an operations request.

documents.title: Decision tree — user request → call flow
documents.content:
# Delegation decision tree

## 1. Data retrieval / status check
- Single fact query → `knowledge_fact_query`.
- Free-text query → `knowledge_query`.
- File read → `knowledge_file_read` (when path is known) / `knowledge_file_list` (for exploration).
- List members / skills / processes / bundles → `member_list` / `skill_list` / `process_list` / `bundle_installed`.
- Config value → `config_get`.

## 2. One-shot execution
- Run a specific skill once = `task_create({skill:'<id>', args:{...}, title})` → `task_run`.
- Check result = `task_get({id})` / `task_list({state:'completed'})`.

## 3. Multi-step workflow
- When a defined process exists = `process_start({id})` (preferred).
- When no definition exists, define with `process_save` then `process_start`.
- Pause for human approval = `process_approve` / `process_resume`.
- Check progress = `process_get({id})` / `process_runs({id})`.

## 4. Direct member agent call
- One-shot query to a specific member = `agent_ask({agentId:'<member_id>', message, ...})`.
- Conversation history = `agent_get_history({agentId})`.
- Routing recommendation = `agent_route({intent})`.

## Priority order
When multiple tools could produce the same result: **process > task > direct tool call > agent_ask**.
- Using a process automatically provides human approval, re-execution, and audit log.
- A 1:1 skill tool only performs the actions defined by that skill — new flows should be wrapped in a process.
- `agent_ask` is reserved for cases that require reasoning delegation. Simple tool calls should be made directly by the manager.

documents.title: Member creation / attachment flow
documents.content:
# Member flow

## New agent member
```
member_create_agent({
  id: 'editor.junior',
  displayName: 'Junior Editor',
  profile: '<profile_id>',
  model: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6' },
  skills: ['content_review', 'ad_publish']
})
```

## Person member
`member_add_person({id, displayName, contact})` — for channel notifications / approvals.

## Workspace attach / detach
- Pull from global pool = `member_attach({memberId})`.
- Remove = `member_detach({memberId})`.
- Global list = `member_global_list`.

## Channel credentials
`member_capture_auth({memberId, channel:'slack'|'discord'|...})` — stores token in Keychain.

documents.title: Skill definition flow
documents.content:
# Skill flow

## New skill
`skill_save({id, scope:'workspace'|'agent', body:{description, inputSchema, action}})`.

## Auto-generate
`skill_generate({goal:'...natural language goal...'})` — generates skill definition candidates via LLM. User confirms, then `skill_save`.

## 3-level scope
- **template** — provided by seed / bundle.
- **workspace** — project's `skills/*.yaml`. `skill_save({scope:'workspace'})`.
- **agent** — per-member overlay. `skill_save({scope:'agent', agentId})`.

## Invocation
1:1 skill tool — each skill id is used directly as the tool name. `skill_list` shows the currently available ids.

## Deletion
`skill_delete({id, scope})`. Clean up agent overlays first, then delete the workspace definition.

documents.title: Process (workflow) flow
documents.content:
# Process flow

## Definition
`process_save({id, name, steps:[{name, skill, args, requiresApproval}], goals})` — multi-step workflow.

## Execution
`process_start({id, args})` → returns run id.
- Approval step = advance with `process_approve({runId})`.
- Cancel = `process_cancel({runId})`.
- Resume = `process_resume({runId})`.

## Retrieval
- Definition list = `process_list`.
- Run history = `process_runs({id})`.
- Single run = `process_get({runId})`.

## Patterns
- Recurring operations (weekly publish · daily report) → wrap in a process and schedule (task_create + recurring).
- Human approval branch → set `requiresApproval: true` on the relevant step.
- LLM reasoning step → the step's skill calls `agent_ask` internally.

sources.title: Ops 5 facades — knowledge / skill / profile / philosophy / fact
sources.description: The meaning and storage layout of each of Ops's 5 knowledge facades. The basis for semantic distinction when ops.admin composes delegation / retrieval flows.

documents.title: Knowledge facade — fact graph + file store
documents.content:
# Knowledge facade

Free-form knowledge for a project. Two kinds:

## 1. Fact (graph node)
- Based on mcp_fact_graph.
- `knowledge_fact_save({subject, predicate, object, source})` — stores a triple.
- `knowledge_fact_query({subject?, predicate?, object?})` — matches triples.
- Used for LLM reasoning, indexing, and summarization.

## 2. File
- Arbitrary files in the project's `knowledge/` folder.
- `knowledge_file_write({path, content})` / `knowledge_file_read({path})` / `knowledge_file_list({prefix})` / `knowledge_file_delete({path})`.
- `knowledge_ingest_file({path})` — ingests an external file into this facade.
- `knowledge_query({q})` — free-text search (over the index).

documents.title: Skill facade — MCP tools + action bodies
documents.content:
# Skill facade

MCP tools that the project or its members can invoke. YAML definition + action body (script · prompt · tool chain).

- `skill_list` / `skill_get` / `skill_save` / `skill_delete` / `skill_generate` / `skill_global_list`.
- Scope = template / workspace / agent (see skill_lifecycle in ops_delegation).
- Invocation = skill id is the tool name as-is. Use `skill_list` output to confirm available ids.

documents.title: Profile facade — agent persona
documents.content:
# Profile facade

Persona definition for agent members (voice tone · values · operational context).

- Location = project `profiles/<id>.yaml`.
- Assigned via `profile: '<id>'` when calling `member_create_agent`.
- Multiple agents can share the same profile.
- Merged as part of the LLM system prompt.

documents.title: Philosophy facade — editorial / operational principles
documents.content:
# Philosophy facade

Editorial principles / operational values for the project — the axis an agent should follow when making decisions.

- Location = project `philosophies/<id>.yaml`.
- 4-axis structure (axis name · scale · principles · examples).
- A profile can reference a philosophy → becomes the agent's decision basis.
- Host comparison tool = `philosophy_compare` (Ops UI); agent receives it merged into systemPrompt.

documents.title: Unified index of the 5 facades
documents.content:
# Unified index

KnowledgeInit.boot attaches all 5 facades.
- Free-text search = `knowledge_query` (merges indexes across all 5 facades).
- Single fact retrieval = `knowledge_fact_query`.
- Per-domain list = `*_list` tool for each facade.
- External LLM = accesses seed manual via `studio://knowledge/ops_*` resource (host fan-out).

sources.title: Ops browser primitive recipes (9 ops)
sources.description: Usage patterns for the 9 host browser tools (browser.page_view · browser.web_search · browser.extract · browser.crawl · browser.monitor · browser.submit_form · browser.download · browser.page_audit_role · browser.page_compare_actors).

documents.title: Meaning of the 9 primitives
documents.content:
# 9 browser primitives

| name | purpose |
|---|---|
| `browser.page_view` | Render a URL page + capture DOM (commonly the first step in a skill). |
| `browser.web_search` | Search engine query. |
| `browser.extract` | Page + selector → structured data. |
| `browser.crawl` | Page + depth → URL tree. |
| `browser.monitor` | URL diff watcher. |
| `browser.submit_form` | Form input / submission (auth · CRUD). |
| `browser.download` | File / media download. |
| `browser.page_audit_role` | Compare a page from a role perspective (UX QA). |
| `browser.page_compare_actors` | Diff the same page across two actors (permission / persona QA). |

All run on the host's shared `browser.*` capability (one mcp_browser engine, owned by the host — built-ins use it, never their own).

documents.title: Research + reporting pattern
documents.content:
# Research flow

```
browser.web_search({query})   // collect candidate URLs
  ↓
browser.page_view({url})      // capture page body
  ↓
browser.extract({url, selector})  // structure the data
  ↓
knowledge_save({...}) /
knowledge_fact_save({...})    // persist to facade
  ↓
skill 'research_and_report'   // generate report (recommended inside a process)
```

documents.title: Publish / channel flow
documents.content:
# Publish flow

```
browser.submit_form({url, fields})  // CMS login or post creation
  ↓
browser.download({url, savePath})   // retrieve attachments or assets
  ↓
channel_notify({channelId, payload})  // Slack / Discord notification
```

The skill `ad_publish` wraps this flow. When defined as a multi-step process, a human approval step can be inserted.

sources.title: Ops tool catalog — actual tool surface on the host endpoint
sources.description: The tool surface visible to ops.admin and external LLM clients — tools registered by McpInbound.registerToolsOn plus 1:1 skill tools.

documents.title: config_*
documents.content:
- `config_get` — full OpsConfig JSON (workspacesRoot · activeWorkspace · llm · mcp · ...).
- `config_reload` — re-reads `~/.makemind-ops/config.yaml`.
- `config_set_llm_provider` · `config_set_mcp_outbound` · `config_set_storage` · `config_set_chromium` — partial updates.
- `system_agent_set_model` — swaps `config.systemAgent.model` only (runtime).

documents.title: workspace_*
documents.content:
- `workspace_list` — sub-workspaces in the current project (slugs under `workspacesRoot`).
- `workspace_create({name, owner})` · `workspace_update` · `workspace_rename` · `workspace_delete` · `workspace_switch({slug})`.

documents.title: member_*
documents.content:
- `member_list` — members of the current workspace.
- `member_global_list` — global pool across all projects.
- `member_get({id})` · `member_create_agent({...})` · `member_add_person({...})` · `member_update` · `member_delete`.
- `member_attach({memberId})` · `member_detach({memberId})`.
- `member_capture_auth({memberId, channel})`.

documents.title: task_* + process_*
documents.content:
- task: `task_list({state?})` · `task_get({id})` · `task_create({title, skill, args, assigneeId?})` · `task_update` · `task_run({id})` · `task_cancel` · `task_delete` · `task_runs`.
- process: `process_list` · `process_get({id})` · `process_save({...})` · `process_start({id, args?})` · `process_approve` · `process_resume` · `process_cancel` · `process_delete` · `process_runs({id})`.

documents.title: knowledge_* + skill_* + bundle_*
documents.content:
- knowledge: `knowledge_query` · `knowledge_save` · `knowledge_fact_save` · `knowledge_fact_query` · `knowledge_file_write` · `knowledge_file_read` · `knowledge_file_list` · `knowledge_file_delete` · `knowledge_ingest_file`.
- skill: `skill_list` · `skill_get` · `skill_global_list` · `skill_save` · `skill_delete` · `skill_generate`. Plus 1:1 tools named after each registered skill id.
- bundle: `bundle_list` · `bundle_installed` · `bundle_install` · `bundle_uninstall`.
- opspack: `opspack_export` · `opspack_import` — Ops package import/export.
- recipe: `recipe_list` · `recipe_seed` — seed recipes.
- form: host `form.*` (`form.create_document` · `form.render` · `form.list_templates` · …) — document generation from templates, on the host endpoint.
- channel: `channel_notify` — Slack / Discord / Web push.
- status: `status_snapshot` — operations snapshot JSON.
- diagnostic: `diagnostic_export` — diagnostic export.
- html_report: `html_report_export` — HTML report export.

documents.title: agent_* + 9 browser primitives
documents.content:
- agent: `agent_ask({agentId, message})` · `agent_assign_skill` · `agent_get_history` · `agent_route({intent})`.
- browser (9, host capability): `browser.page_view` · `browser.page_audit_role` · `browser.web_search` · `browser.extract` · `browser.crawl` · `browser.monitor` · `browser.submit_form` · `browser.download` · `browser.page_compare_actors`.

Additional: ui_* (debug surface), makemind-ops:// resources (external MCP guide / concepts / workflows / tool-catalog / live-state) and prompts (getting_started · drive_workspace · author_skill · evolve_agent · compose_process).

agents.name: Ops Admin
agents.systemPrompt:
You are the manager for Makemind Ops — a vibe_studio built-in app for project operations. The user talks to you whenever the Ops tab is active. You speak the user's language (Korean if they write in Korean).

Identity:
  • One Ops tab = one project directory. All operational data (members / tasks / skills / processes / profiles / philosophies / knowledge) lives inside that project.
  • Seed assets = you (`ops.admin`) + the operations manual (`studio://knowledge/ops_overview/*` · `ops_delegation/*` · `ops_concepts/*` · `ops_browser_recipes/*` · `ops_tool_catalog/*`).
  • Project members (per-project agents) are separate from you — each has its own LLM, skill whitelist, and persona.

When a project is NOT bound:
  • Guide the user to the wizard's `New project` / `Open project`. The Ops wizard routes through the host chrome bridge slot.
  • Do not try to create a directory yourself — the host chrome's standard wiring handles it.

When a project IS bound — routing rules:
  1. Assess state first — the first action is `config_get` plus the needed `member_list` / `skill_list` / `process_list` / `bundle_installed`.
  2. Simple query / change → call the tool directly (you have authority over the entire Ops tool surface).
  3. Multi-step operations → if a defined process exists, prefer `process_start`. Otherwise define it with `process_save`, then `process_start`.
  4. One-off skill execution → `task_create({skill, args})` → `task_run`.
  5. Delegate reasoning / review to a member → `agent_ask({agentId:'<member_id>', message})`. For free-form intent → recommend candidates with `agent_route`.
  6. Web research → 9 host browser primitives (`browser.page_view` · `browser.web_search` · `browser.extract` · ...) → save with `knowledge_save` when needed.
  7. Do not guess identifiers — confirm any unknown `id` with a list tool first.

Cross-builtin:
  • Other tools on the vibe_studio host endpoint (`studio.*` · `app_builder.*` · `scene.*` · `bk.*`) are exposed on the same surface. Call them only when truly needed, and prioritize your own domain (Ops).
  • The host manager (`studio.manager`) is responsible for routing other built-ins. When the user makes a request like "I want to build an app", guide them to the host manager / app_builder.

Writing:
  • Answer in markdown. For tool results, attach a one-line summary the user can act on plus only the raw output that's needed.
  • If ambiguous, ask once and proceed (especially for destructive actions: delete · cancel · uninstall).
